draft: Before you fill in the fields below, work the table on paper the way you would on the exam: list its functional dependencies, then walk 1NF → 2NF → 3NF. The graded answers should just be reading off your work.

fields.label: This wide table is in 1NF (every cell is atomic). What is the first normal form it violates?
The key is the composite (order_id, product). Which non-key columns depend on only PART of that key (on order_id alone) — the partial dependencies?
List the functional dependencies among the non-key columns — the ones that make each employee, branch, and client fact repeat on every order line.
fields.help: One per line, in the form  a -> b  (a composite left side is written  a, b -> c).

fields.label: After normalizing all the way to 3NF, which table does each fact live in? Drop each column into its home table.
fields.help: Keys and foreign keys are already placed for you; these are the descriptive facts.
